description: Phase 6 — Polish & ship (PLAN.md §9 final phase): the cross-platform distribution + quality pass that turns the feature-complete app (Core Parity v1.0 + Fast-follow v1.1 + AI v1.2) into a signed, auto-updating, accessible, performance-budgeted product. Covers perf benchmarking + budgets (criterion on large synthetic repos; cold-open < ~1s to first paint, status refresh < ~100ms warm, 60fps graph scroll — PLAN.md §8.9), large-repo perf passes against any misses, accessibility (keyboard nav, focus, ARIA, contrast in light+dark), theming polish (finalize tokens + reduced-motion), packaging via the Tauri bundler for macOS (.dmg/.app, notarized), Windows (.msi/NSIS, Authenticode), and Linux (AppImage + Flatpak), auto-update via the Tauri updater plugin with signed manifests, user/keyboard/privacy/MCP docs + release notes, a release CI pipeline (build → sign → notarize → publish artifacts + update manifest on tag, cross-platform matrix), and the GA exit gate. Builds on the existing CI (fmt/clippy/test/deny + npm build, macOS/Windows/Linux matrix from Phase 0) and the bundle config stub (src-tauri/tauri.conf.json bundle.active=false). No new product features — this is the ship. Each story = one context-window commit. Secrets (signing certs/keys, notarization creds, updater private key) live in CI secrets / the OS keychain, NEVER in the repo; the updater public key may be committed. Version line: workspace is at 1.2.0 after Phase 5; the GA ship bumps to 1.3.0.

userStories.title: Performance benchmarks + budgets
userStories.description: As the team, I need measured performance budgets so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: criterion benchmarks for the hot pure paths (graph layout in lady-graph, diff in lady-diff, log walk + status in lady-git) over a large synthetic repo fixture (linux.git-scale generator, deterministic seed)
Documented budgets (PLAN.md §8.9): cold open of a large repo < ~1s to first paint, status refresh < ~100ms on warm cache, 60fps graph scroll; a bench harness prints current vs budget
A CI job runs the benches in --quick/check mode (no perf gate failure on shared runners, but the harness compiles + runs); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green; cargo deny passes
Results recorded in docs (a short PERF.md or a section) with the measured numbers on the dev machine
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §8.9, §10 perf. Establish the measurement before optimizing (PH6-002). Use a seeded synthetic-repo generator in a dev-dependency/test helper so benches are reproducible and offline.

userStories.title: Large-repo performance passes
userStories.description: As a user with a big repository, I want Lady to stay fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Address any budget misses found in PH6-001: confirm incremental/stable graph layout streams rows (no full re-layout on scroll/refresh), commit list + diff views virtualize, log walk is paged, status uses a warm cache
No O(history) work on the UI thread for a single screen of rows; large diffs lazy-load hunks; image/binary diffs are bounded
Re-run the PH6-001 benches and show the budgets are met (or document the residual gap + why); cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
Manual window check on a large repo (e.g. a clone of a big OSS repo): scroll, refresh, open diffs
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §8.1/§8.9. Mostly verification + targeted fixes — much of this is already incremental from Phase 1. Only optimize what the benches show is over budget.

userStories.title: Accessibility pass
userStories.description: As a keyboard or screen-reader user, I want Lady to be usable without a mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Keyboard navigation across all views (tab order, focus rings, Esc/Enter semantics in dialogs, the command palette reachable + operable by keyboard); no mouse-only actions
Semantic roles/labels on controls (buttons, tabs, lists, the diff/graph regions) so a screen reader announces them; live regions for streaming AI output + progress
Color contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark themes; focus is always visible; prefers-reduced-motion respected
Documented manual a11y check (VoiceOver/NVDA pass over the core flows); npm run build green; cargo fmt/clippy/test green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §6 UX, §8. SolidJS + WebView makes ARIA/contrast tractable (ADR-0001/0010). No backend change expected — UI-only.

userStories.title: Theming polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want a consistent, polished, themeable UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Finalize the CSS theme-token set (surfaces, borders, accent, syntax colors) so every view uses tokens — no hard-coded colors leak through in light or dark (audit the views added in Phases 1-5)
System/Dark/Light toggle (PH from Phase 4 dark mode) verified across all views incl. the new AI surfaces; reduced-motion honored
Optional: a custom-accent or extra theme to prove the token system; documented
npm run build green; cargo fmt/clippy/test green; manual window check across themes
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §6. Builds on the existing System/Dark/Light toggle + themed syntax highlighting (recent main commits). Audit + close gaps rather than rebuild.

userStories.title: Packaging — macOS (.dmg/.app, notarized)
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want a signed, notarized installable build.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri bundler config produces a .app + .dmg (bundle.active=true with macOS targets, identifier, category, icons already present)

Developer ID code-signing + notarization wired via CI secrets (signing identity, Apple ID/app-specific password or notarytool API key) — NEVER committed; a Gatekeeper-pass is documented (spctl/stapler)
A local/CI build produces an installable artifact; the build step is green on the macOS runner; cargo fmt/clippy/test green
Signing/notarization is skipped gracefully when secrets are absent (PR builds still produce an unsigned bundle)
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §8.8, §9. ADR-0001 Tauri bundler. Secrets in CI only. Notarization is the macOS Gatekeeper requirement; stapling the ticket to the .dmg.

userStories.title: Packaging — Windows (.msi/NSIS, Authenticode)
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want a signed installer.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri bundler produces an .msi and/or NSIS installer on the Windows runner
Authenticode signing wired via a CI secret cert (or Azure Trusted Signing) — NEVER committed; signature verified (signtool verify) and documented
The build step is green on the Windows runner; signing skipped gracefully without secrets; cargo fmt/clippy/test green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §8.8, §9. Mirror the macOS story for Windows. Keep signing optional on forks/PRs.

userStories.title: Packaging — Linux (AppImage + Flatpak)
userStories.description: As a Linux user, I want portable installable builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri bundler produces an AppImage on the Linux runner; a Flatpak manifest builds the app
The artifacts launch (smoke-documented) and bundle the WebKitGTK runtime deps correctly
The build step is green on the Linux runner; cargo fmt/clippy/test green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §8.8, §9. Linux has no code-signing requirement; focus on portability + runtime deps (the gtk-rs advisories are already deny-ignored as not-runtime-reachable elsewhere, but Linux IS the runtime here — verify launch).

userStories.title: Auto-update (Tauri updater + signed manifests)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to update itself safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri updater plugin wired: the app checks a signed update manifest/endpoint, prompts the user, and applies a verified update
Update manifests are signed with the updater private key (CI secret); the PUBLIC key is embedded/committed; an unsigned/tampered manifest is rejected
An in-app 'check for updates' + update-available flow; update only on explicit user action (no silent forced update)
Unit/integration coverage where feasible (manifest signature verify); cargo fmt/clippy/test green; cargo deny passes; npm run build green
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §8.8 Auto-update, §9. ADR-0001 Tauri updater. Signature verification is trust-critical — only the public key is committed; the private key signs manifests in CI.

userStories.title: Documentation + release notes
userStories.description: As a new user, I want docs to get started and understand privacy.
acceptanceCriteria: A user guide (open/clone, the core flows, command palette, keyboard shortcuts reference), an AI/privacy doc (BYOK, consent, redaction-is-best-effort, the local Ollama path — ADR-0008/0009), and an MCP setup doc (lady-mcp, read-only, configuring an external assistant)
Release notes / CHANGELOG covering Core Parity → Fast-follow → AI → ship (v1.0 → 1.3.0)
Docs build/lint clean (markdown); links resolve; no secrets in docs
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §9 docs. Pull from CONTEXT.md, the ADRs, and progress notes. Keyboard-shortcuts doc complements the PH6-003 a11y work.

userStories.title: Release CI pipeline
userStories.description: As the team, I want tagging a release to produce signed artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: A tag-triggered release workflow builds on the macOS/Windows/Linux matrix, runs the full green gate (fmt/clippy -D warnings/test/deny + npm build), then bundles + signs/notarizes per OS (reusing PH6-005/006/007) and publishes the artifacts + the signed update manifest (PH6-008)
All signing/notarization/updater secrets come from CI secrets; the workflow is a no-op-safe on forks (no secrets → unsigned artifacts, no publish)
A dry-run (workflow_dispatch) produces artifacts without publishing; documented
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §9, §10 cross-platform CI. Extends the existing .github/workflows/ci.yml (build+test+deny matrix from Phase 0) with a release.yml. Keep PR CI unchanged + fast.

userStories.title: Phase 6 GA exit gate (v1.3.0 ship)
userStories.description: As the team, I want to confirm Lady is shippable and cut GA.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/RELEASE-CHECKLIST.md updated: perf budgets, accessibility, theming, packaging (macOS/Windows/Linux), auto-update, docs, release CI — each ticked to its story; the GA ship line recorded
Whole workspace green: cargo fmt --all --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo deny check, npm run build; release workflow produces signed artifacts on the matrix
Version bumped to 1.3.0 across all crates + tauri.conf.json + ui/package.json; notes confirm GA — Core Parity + Fast-follow + AI + a signed, auto-updating, accessible, perf-budgeted ship
This is the final planned phase; any remaining items are explicitly listed as post-GA
userStories.notes: PLAN.md §9 Phase 6 exit — the ship. Version line: workspace is at 1.2.0 after Phase 5; bump to 1.3.0 here. Mirror the Phase 4/5 exit-gate stories.
